content.question:
Thanks.



Has this been aligned with @Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com> and @Ning
Li <ning.li@shopee.com>?

Slide 2

   - Not quite sure how to read the tables on this slide in general



Slide 3

   - SIP I get it but we should have product expertise for SBS/Lovito/SCS?
There should be no excuse there of not knowing our products?

Slide 5

   - How do we measure the Shop Conversion rate here?
- What is this latest LLM chatbot that we are referring to?

Is this the
   shop chat bot that certain sellers have started to use?

What is the roll
   out coverage for this so far?
Which markets?

What type of sellers has the
   usage been?

Slide 7

   - So we will have the latest shop chat bot for all business lines by for
   local by Apr and CB by May?

- There are probably steps to enhance the answers of the chatbot as we
   should have a good sense of buyer intent by now?

What are the steps that we
   can take?

Slide 10

   - What does CB SID mean here?

Slide 13 – 14

   - It is not so clear to me
   - For each of these business lines on Slide 13, how do we see the
   reporting structure moving forward?

Who will be in charge of managing the
   performance of the agents (no matter where they may be)
   - Then the next question would be for each of these business lines,
   where would the agents be?

Slide 17

   - Are we intending to split up pre-sale and post-sale agents?
- Or we intend for all agents to be able to handle pre-sale as well?

Slide 19 – 21

   - Not quite sure what this means

content.answer:
Hi Ian,


Has this been aligned with @Shuning Wang and @Ning Li?

   -

   They are aligned, this plan has been created in collaboration with their
   teams.


Slide 2

   -

   Not quite sure how to read the tables on this slide in general
   -

   In general, there is potential to generate revenue uplift by increasing
   the contact rate to align with top external sellers and boost the absolute
   number of conversions.
   -

   While our order conversion is higher than external sellers, our GMV
   conversion value is much lower. We need to high-value items as well.

[image: image.png]




Slide 3

   -

   SIP I get it but we should have product expertise for SBS/Lovito/SCS?
   There should be no excuse there of not knowing our products?
   -

   Ok. We can do for SBS/SCS/Lovio.



Slide 5

   -

   How do we measure the Shop Conversion rate here?

Converted order = order placed within 24 hrs after a conversation

Formula: Shop Conversion rate = Converted order / # Conversation *100%

   -

   What is this latest LLM chatbot that we are referring to? Is this the
   shop chat bot  that certain sellers have started to use? What is the roll
   out coverage for this so far? Which markets? What type of sellers has the
   usage been?
   -

      Yes, the latest LLM chatbot is the one most advanced version which
      the external sellers have started to use
      -

      [By 22 Mar] Have rolled out to selected sellers in all markets,
      covers both managed and unmanaged, covers all 4 L0 categories (FMCG,
      Fashion, Lifestyle, EL) - no detailed breakdown. Can see the
below data of
      seller adoption
      -

      How to push adoption: (1) understand seller’s motivation and barriers
      better from seller’s feedback; (2) closely communicate the unique
      capabilities of chatbot to attract sellers; (3) continuously improve
      the chatbot functionalities to meet seller’s requirem

Hi Ian,

Here are the next steps we discussed in today's meeting. Feel free to let
us know if you have anything to add.

Next Steps for Biz Owner

   - Business owners (Shunning and Li Ning) are responsible for the
   business and its CS, including agent performance. For local SCS/SBS, the
   responsibility lies with the local SCS/SBS business owner. The business
   owner should take ownership of the metrics and adjust reporting lines as
   soon as possible
   - Business owners to focus on driving business, not just cost. In terms
   of cost, to follow this order of priority:


   1.

      Improve productivity, provide plan in the next update (within 1-2
      weeks)
      2.

      Reduce contact rate via Chatbot; to work with Reg CS to ensure
      rollout doesn’t impact performance significant; proceed only when ready
      3.

      Move CB SCS and SIP agents to PH, to update on timeline in next
      update


Next Steps for Reg CS


Reg CS to analyze conversion rate/metrics

   1.

      Contact rate differences between us and the benchmark; determine the
      target equilibrium rate
      2.

      Shopchat bot seller adoption rates by ADO and GMV (in addition to
      shop numbers)
      3.

      Reg CS to assess GMV uplift from conversations, i.e. by comparing ABS
      values of conversations vs. non-conversations
      4.

      To provide update by next update in (early May)


   -

   Reg CS to learn from Ian’s team and apply best practices across markets,
   handling cross-cutting topics (e.g., driving conversions, pre-sales split,
   org. model)
   -

   Reg CS to support business owners with systems, tools, and performance
   tracking



Best Regards
Steven Shi

On Fri, Apr 4, 2025 at 1:53 PM Shi Cong Steven <steven.shi@shopee.com>
wrote:

content.question:
Thanks.



Can help align on a standardized metric that all Shop CS (across all
various business and countries) should be using?

Maybe can send across
sometime early next week?

So business will know what to follow and act on.



Can also look at agent KPIs next based on the standardized metrics above?

We should also be able to standardize this across business and markets.



*From:* Shi Cong Steven <steven.shi@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Friday, 4 April 2025 9:27 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Eric Bui <eric.bui@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>;
Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com>; Vivian Zhai <vivian.zhai@shopee.com>; Joy
Shen Xijiao <joy.shenxj@shopee.com>; Harshit Arora <harshit.arora@shopee.com>;
Weifeng Ng <weifeng.ng@shopee.com>; Bobby Muljono <bobby.muljono@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: <Pre-read> Follow-up on Shop CS from Reg CS



Hi Ian,



Here are the next steps we discussed in today's meeting. Feel free to let
us know if you have anything to add.



*Next Steps for Biz Owner *

   - Business owners (Shunning and Li Ning) are responsible for the
   business and its CS, including agent performance. For local SCS/SBS, the
   responsibility lies with the local SCS/SBS business owner. The business
   owner should take ownership of the metrics and adjust reporting lines as
   soon as possible
   - Business owners to focus on driving business, not just cost. In terms
   of cost, to follow this order of priority:

a.             Improve productivity, provide plan in the next update
(within 1-2 weeks)

b.             Reduce contact rate via Chatbot; to work with Reg CS to
ensure rollout doesn’t impact performance significant; proceed only when
ready

c.              Move CB SCS and SIP agents to PH, to update on timeline in
next update



*Next Steps for Reg CS *



Reg CS to analyze conversion rate/metrics

a.             Contact rate differences between us and the benchmark;
determine the target equilibrium rate

b.             Shopchat bot seller adoption rates by ADO and GMV (in
addition to shop numbers)

c.              Reg CS to assess GMV uplift from conversations, i.e. by
comparing ABS values of conversations vs. non-conversations

d.             To provide update by next update in (early May)

   - Reg CS to learn from Ian’s team and apply best practices across
   markets, handling cross-cutting topics (e.g., driving conversions,
   pre-sales split, org. model)
   - Reg CS to support business owners with systems, tools, and performance
   tracking





Best Regards

Steven Shi



On Fri, Apr 4, 2025 at 1:53 PM Shi Cong Steven <steven.shi@shopee.com>
wrote:

Hi Ian,



Has this been aligned with @Shuning Wang and @Ning Li?

- They are aligned, this plan has been created in collaboration with
   their teams.



Slide 2

   - Not quite sure how to read the tables on this slide in general
   - In general, there is potential to generate revenue uplift by
   increasing the contact rate to align with top external sellers and boost
   the absolute number of conversions.
   - While our order conversion is higher than external sellers, our GMV
   conversion value is much lower. We need to high-value items as well.





Slide 3

   - SIP I get it but we should have product expertise for SBS/Lovito/SCS?
There should be no excuse there of not knowing our products?

- Ok. We can do for SBS/SCS/Lovio.



Slide 5

   - How do we measure the Shop Conversion rate here?

Converted order = order placed within 24 hrs after a conversation

Formula: Shop Conversion rate = Converted order / # Conversation *100%

   - What is this latest LLM chatbot that we are referring to?

Is this the
   shop chat bot  that certain sellers have started to use?

What is the roll
   out coverage for this so far?
Which markets?

What type of sellers has the
   usage been?

- Yes, the latest LLM chatbot is the one most advanced version which the
      external sellers have started to use
      - [By 22 Mar] Have rolled out to selected sellers in all markets,
      covers both managed and unmanaged, covers all 4 L0 categories (FMCG,
      Fashion, Lifestyle, EL) - no detailed breakdown. Can see the
below data of
      seller adoption
      - *How to push adoption: (1) *understand seller’s motivation and
      barriers better from seller’s feedback; *(2)* closely communicate the
      unique capabilities of chatbot to attract sellers; *(3) *continuously
      improve the chatbot functionalities to meet seller’s requirements.
      -



Slide 7

So we will have the latest shop chat bot for all business lines by for
local by Apr and CB by May?

o   Yes.

§  The local timeline is more confirmed since they are in the rolled out
stage.

§  CB is facing uncertainty from current AB testing, but we are targeting
by May and will also try to push it earlier.

   - There are probably steps to enhance the answers of the chatbot as we
   should have a good sense of buyer intent by now?

What are the steps that we
   can take?

- Buyer intent recognition still needs further improvement, so two
      aspects for enhancing chatbot answer:


   - *(1) Biz:* optimize chatbot answer for the questions that not covered
         by LLM model yet; enrich seller knowledge base (mainly
product info) to
         support model improvement.
         - *(2) Tech*: improve intent recognition model; improve data
         mining capabilities for chatbot answer (e.g. bot to get
answer from seller
         chat history); rollout more chatbot features for improving pre-sale
         capabilities (e.g., add-to-cart reminder) and post-sales
(leverage MP CS
         bot capability to answer)



Slide 10

   - What does CB SID mean here?

- SID - ID sellers selling to SG, MY, and TH and agents are sitting in
   ID to manage the chats



Slide 13 – 14

   - It is not so clear to me
   - For each of these business lines on Slide 13, how do we see the
   reporting structure moving forward?

Who will be in charge of managing the
   performance of the agents (no matter where they may be)
   - Then the next question would be for each of these business lines,
   where would the agents be?

- Biz line owners should be directly in charge of agents, i.e. Russel
   (CB SCS), Qiang Wei (SIP), and Saru (Local SCS/SBS).
   - For Russel (CB SCS), more straightforward as all agents are located in
   VN.
   - For Qiang Wei (SIP), more complicated as agents are distributed in
   respective buyer markets. Propose to centralise, in PH, if ID is not
   possible by this year.
   - For Saru (Local SCS/SBS), agents can be centralised in ID while the
   reporting line is still pending as Local SCS suppliers and Warehouse are
   all located locally.



Slide 17

   - Are we intending to split up pre-sale and post-sale agents?
- Or we intend for all agents to be able to handle pre-sale as well?

Hi Ian,

Refer to the attached deck for key Shopchat metrics. We have selected* 5
key Reg metrics (outlined on slide 2) *and will collaborate with the
business owners to set targets for them.


These metrics and its definition have been aligned with the CB business
owners. For Local SCS/SBS, alignment has been done with the regional CS
team within SPX. Moving forward, we will also engage with the relevant
business line owners for local SCS/SBS across markets.


Best Regards
Steven Shi

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 2

   - I think we need to split up Agent and Chatbot performance much clearer?

- For agent side, we should look at
      - CSAT
      - Average waiting time in queue
      - Abandon rate
      - Productivity
      - Conversion rate
      - Cost per chat (probably later date)
   - For chatbot, we should look at
      - Deflection Rate
      - CSAT



Others

   - Can see how the above measure against MP CS OKRs?

Can see what is
   missing?

- Based on the above
      - How do we bucket the individual Agent KPI?
- How does each business and lane fare?

Including local SG/MY SBS
      - What are the targets moving forward?

+ @hoirul.hafiidz@shopee.com <hoirul.hafiidz@shopee.com>
@esten.mok@shopee.com <esten.mok@shopee.com>

*Sent:* Sunday, 13 April 2025 9:15 am

Others

   Can see how the above measure against MP CS OKRs? Can see what is
   missing?
   -

   We’ve compared Shop CS metrics against those for MP CS in slide 2,
   generally
   -

   What’s missing: We're currently unable to measure E2E metrics for Shop
   CS, including related metrics like First Contact Resolution (FCR) and % of
   responses within 24 hours. E2E  measures the time from when a case is
   created to when it is *resolved.*
   -

   However, in Shop CS - particularly in pre-sales scenarios - the concept
   of a case being "resolved" is less applicable, as customers often ask
   multiple questions before making a purchase decision. There isn't a
   specific issue to resolve in the traditional sense.

      -

      slides 4, 6, and 8. However, we would not go into individual lanes, as
      agents are organized based on the buyer markets they serve,

content.answer:
Capacity planning challenges – The rollout of the new chatbot (expected
   to significantly improve deflection rates) was delayed. This rollout is now
   scheduled for April 21 and is expected to reduce AR and wait time
   significantly.

Agent concurrency adjustment – In an effort to improve CSAT, maximum
   agent concurrency was reduced from 5 to 3 last week. This has since been
   reverted back to 5, as it impacted capacity.

Staffing shortages – A recent loss of 6 agents due to resignations
   created a headcount gap, further straining the team. MY will increase HC
   numbers in the upcoming weeks.

Despite these challenges, performance has improved since last week


   -

   Abandonment rates dropped from 5.2% to 1.5%


   -

   Average wait times improved from 65 minutes 45 minutes, expected to
   reach under 3 minutes by week of 5th May



Slide 9 <Individual Agent KPIs>

   -

   These agents are expected to perform outbound tasks?
   -

   Yes, agents are expected to conduct outbound follow-ups to address cases
   that come in during off-hours. For MP CS, these are typically handled by
   dedicated “email” agents or mitigated through 24/7 chat support. However,
   since Shop CS doesn't have a similar agent type and a large portion of
   cases require outbound handling, it’s important for us to track this metric
   for Shop CS agents.



Others

   -

   I wonder if it make sense to reshuffle HC between SG and MY businesses.
   Some teams feel like no need  people, some need more..

There are several key hurdles to overcome before combining operations for
SG and MY (as below). For now, we relook into WFM to cover the immediate
gaps first. Hurdles include:


   -

   ROI: Cross-market agent sharing may not be efficient, as frequent
   switching would likely require substantial retraining, increased costs and
   offsetting gains from improved utilisation.

   -

   User Experience: There's a risk of agents applying the wrong market's
   SOPs, which could lead to inconsistent or incorrect customer support.

   -

   Ownership/Structural Issues: The team overseeing the shared agents may
   prioritize their own market’s performance, potentially to the detriment of
   the other.

Nevertheless, as we explore the possibility of having a dedicated group of
pre-sales agents, we will consider combining operations and optimizing b

content.answer:
Case follow-ups
      -

         An inbound contact is received from the user during the day which
         is responded to by the agent within the same day
         -

         However, the user issue is still not resolved and he/she follows
         up on the same case a few days later
         -

         Agent responds to this query with an outbound contact to address
         user’s follow-up concern/clarifications

*Enquiry during non-working hours*
      -

         An inbound contact is received from the user during late-night
         hours when no agents are available/working. The inquiry is
then followed
         up by an agent the next day as an outbound task.

Do we have targets set for the respective businesses yet for the upcoming
months?



How are we rolling this out for the other markets?

   -

   For Agent metrics, market level targets are not ready yet. We will
   detail out and align on the monthly targets with all markets by next week.
   -

   For Chatbot metrics, we are awaiting the latest LLM model (expected in
   May) to have a more accurate baseline for target setting before rolling out
   to the markets

We’ve already launched a weekly dashboard tracking the performance of these
metrics. Starting next week, we’ll incorporate monthly targets into the
dashboard to give local teams better visibility into MTD performance versus
monthly goals, enabling closer monitoring of their progress toward target
achievement.

Note that for local SCS/SBS, we've prioritized setting/aligning targets for
SG and MY; we'll be able to provide this early next week.


Best regards
Steven Shi

content.answer:
Metrics and Associated Considerations

Shopchat agents’ metrics are similar to that used for MP agents, with a few
key differences:


   -

   For MP agents, we measure handled productivity and solved productivity.
   However, for Shopchat agents, there is no "solved productivity" concept.
   Since outbound activity is considered part of their BAU (business-as-usual)
   tasks, handled productivity is further split into inbound and outbound
   productivity.
   -

   MP agents do not track a Pre-sale Conversion Rate, whereas Shopchat
   agents do. The weightage for this metric can be adjusted based on the
   agent’s role.

Implementation

Applicable for CB SCS, SIP, Local SCS/SBS:

Milestone

Phase 1:  Manual Bucketization and Scoring

Done via Google Sheets, calculated on monthly basis

Phase 2: System-Based Bucketization and Scoring

In-house system will automate process, allowing agents to view daily
performance and grading through online dashboard (akin to MP CS)

Communication to Local Teams

Introduction & briefing on shopchat bucketization on 13 Mar

Feedback gathering from biz. owners

Share manual bucketization working file and dashboard to business owners by
30 April

Finalise Product Requirement Documents (PRD) by 2 May

Training for Roll out

Conduct manual bucketization training by 9 May

Tentatively scheduled for Q3, with final timelines to be confirmed by May
after assessing development efforts.

Target setting

Target setting by biz owners by 16 May

Incentive Plan

Propose incentive plan by mid-May

BPO Communication

Communicate to BPO agents May - Jun 2025

Actual Roll out

June/July 2025


Best Regards
Steven Shi

content.question: Thanks, has this been aligned with the business teams?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Yes, we've aligned with biz. teams for CB SBS, SIP, and local SCS/SBS for
SG and MY.

Note that in Phase 1 (Manual Bucketization and Scoring), MY local SCS/SBS
will use a simplified manual approach without separating inbound and
outbound productivity, as they are launching ahead of the Reg CS
initiative on May 1 and have already communicated metrics and associated
targets to agents. Full alignment with the Reg CS framework is planned for
Phase 2, once systemization is implemented in Q3.

Best Regards
Steven Shi
